records.ety: [Probably fr. OE. pippin a seed, as being raised from the seed. See Pip a seed.]

defs: An apple from a tree raised from the seed and not grafted; a seedling apple.
A name given to apples of several different kinds, as Newtown pippin, summer pippin, fall pippin, golden pippin.
quotes: We will eat a last year's pippin.
